comment_text.sn35.160:1.1: This monastery belonged to the Buddha’s doctor, Jīvaka, who appears in DN 2. His story is told in Kd 8, where we learn that he was raised by Prince Abhaya of Magadha, a Jain (MN 58) who was also interested in the teachings of Pūraṇa Kassapa (SN 46.56). MN 55 on eating meat is addressed to him. | This discourse and the next are similar to SN 22.5–6.

root_text.sn35.160:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati jīvakambavane.
root_text.sn35.160:1.7: Cakkhuṁ aniccanti yathābhūtaṁ okkhāyati, rūpā aniccāti yathābhūtaṁ okkhāyati, cakkhuviññāṇaṁ aniccanti yathābhūtaṁ okkhāyati, cakkhusamphasso aniccoti yathābhūtaṁ okkhāyati, yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi aniccanti yathābhūtaṁ okkhāyati …pe…
root_text.sn35.160:1.9: mano aniccoti yathābhūtaṁ okkhāyati, dhammā aniccāti yathābhūtaṁ okkhāyati …pe…
root_text.sn35.160:1.10: yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi aniccanti yathābhūtaṁ okkhāyati.

translation_text.sn35.160:0.3: On Immersion at Jīvaka’s Mango Grove
translation_text.sn35.160:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha in Jīvaka’s Mango Grove.
translation_text.sn35.160:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn35.160:1.5: For a mendicant with immersion, things become truly clear.
translation_text.sn35.160:1.7: It becomes truly clear that the eye, sights, eye consciousness, and eye contact are impermanent. And it also becomes truly clear that the painful, pleasant, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on eye contact is impermanent.
translation_text.sn35.160:1.8: It becomes truly clear that the ear … nose … tongue … body …
translation_text.sn35.160:1.9: mind, ideas, mind consciousness, and mind contact are impermanent.
translation_text.sn35.160:1.10: And it also becomes truly clear that the painful, pleasant, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact is impermanent.
translation_text.sn35.160:1.12: For a mendicant with immersion, things become truly clear.”
